title: moroccan charred moroccan lamb mechoui
description: a classic moroccan dish featuring tender, flavorful lamb and crusty rye bread. traditionally served at festive gatherings, this recipe is kosher-friendly and makes a perfect addition to any meal.

ingredients.name: rye bread
ingredients.notes: preferably day-old bread for best results

instructions: season the lamb with cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
heat the olive oil in a large dutch oven over medium-high heat. sear the lamb on all sides until browned, about 5 minutes per side.
transfer the lamb to a plate and set aside. add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
add the water to the pot and bring to a boil.
return the lamb to the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
remove the lamb from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving with crusty rye bread and a sprinkle of chopped parsley.
serve warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
